transaction_velocity.notes: Multiple major transactions closed in Q1 2026. Ken Griffin + Goldman Sachs acquired 545Wyn (545 NW 26th St) for $180M — a 10-story, ~500K SF office building. Moishe Mana acquired 1.6-acre assemblage at 2661-2701 NW 5th Ave for $33.5M (approved for 244 units / 489 hotel rooms; up to 612 units under Live Local Act). Wynwood Norte 230-unit multifamily traded for $72M in March 2025. $23.5M mixed-use sale at miamicrebrokers.com. Wynwood/Edgewater had 143 commercial buildings listed for sale as of search date.

distress_level.notes: No elevated distress identified in Wynwood/Edgewater. Miami-Dade metro averages 450-500 foreclosures/month system-wide, but institutional-grade product in this submarket shows no specific distress signals. Zillow shows 391 total listings in Wynwood-Edgewater; 201 metro-wide foreclosures at $740K median. Low distress score reflects favorable acquisition environment (no bargain distress plays, but also no systemic risk).
distress_level.feature_gap: No granular lis pendens pull by zip/neighborhood — Miami-Dade Clerk portal not machine-readable for this zone

supply_risk.notes: Edgewater has 1,317 multifamily units under construction as of February 2026. Wynwood Business Improvement District reports 14+ projects planned with 4 completed in 2025. Notable pipeline: EDITION Residences (55-story, 185 units, breaking ground 2026, Arquitectonica design); Melo Group twin towers (North Tower topped off, South Tower TCO issued); 1600 Edgewater (Sabet Group, 33-story, 282 units, UDRB approved March 2025); Evolve Wynwood 35 (8-story). Transit-oriented 210-unit project adjacent to future light rail stop in Wynwood also under construction.

major_projects.name: Melo Group Twin Towers (Edgewater)
major_projects.status: North Tower topped off; South Tower TCO issued

rent_growth.notes: Wynwood +4.7% YoY as of February 2026. Miami metro overall +0.6% YoY — Wynwood dramatically outperforming. Upper-tier demand driven by white-collar workers ($101,400 avg income). Wynwood Haus (224 units, opened 2025) stabilized at 96% occupancy. Miami dominates Sunbelt rental markets per MIAMI REALTORS March 2026 report. No concessions observed in premium product.

demand_drivers.notes: Miami metro gained highest number of private sector jobs among all metro areas in August 2025 (FloridaCommerce data). Labor force participation 63.8% vs 62.7% national avg. Miami ranked No. 2 state for inbound moves (U-Haul 2025). White-collar tech/finance cluster deepening: Blackstone, Ernst & Young, Uber, Carlton Fields at Brightline MiamiCentral. Ken Griffin/Goldman institutional validation. Major transit catalyst: Miami-Dade $927M public transit project connecting Wynwood/Design District/Little Haiti to downtown via new stations. Hub Miami (245K SF office + 306 condos) under development at Brickell/Edgewater/Wynwood nexus.

major_moves.action: institutional validation via $180M Wynwood office acquisition
$927M transit investment connecting Wynwood to downtown
245K SF office + 306 condos at Edgewater/Wynwood nexus

capital_availability.notes: Blue Owl Capital closed $335M floating-rate bridge loan for Wynwood Plaza (L&L Holding / Oak Row Equities) — refinancing construction debt as project completes. Nuveen Green Capital provided $124.2M C-PACE financing for Wynwood multifamily. CMBS issuance hit record ~$162B in 2025 (+140% YoY). 2026 outlook is 'busier than 2025' with $900B loan maturity wall driving refi activity. Financing conditions improving, spreads tightening. CREFC Miami 2026 conference sentiment: capital available, competition intense.

regulatory_favorability.notes: No rent control proposed or enacted in Miami/Wynwood. Florida state law preempts local rent control. OZ program made permanent via OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025) — Wynwood attracted $2B+ in prior OZ investment, 500+ affordable units under OZ fund. New OBBBA round of OZ designations possible with rural focus. NRD-2 (Wynwood Norte) provides predictable entitlement path with public benefits bonus density. Live Local Act enables 27-story density on qualifying sites. Florida Amendment 5 (Nov 2024) increases homestead exemption to $51,411 — investor-side not directly impacted. No adverse tax reassessment signals.

key_findings.finding: Ken Griffin and Goldman Properties acquired 545Wyn for $180M in January 2026 — the largest office transaction in Wynwood history and a powerful institutional validation of the submarket.
Wynwood rents +4.7% YoY as of February 2026, far outpacing the broader Miami metro (+0.6%) and reversing the national Sunbelt rent correction trend.
Miami-Dade County committed $927M to a new public transit project with stations in Wynwood and the Design District, connecting to downtown via Brightline. This is a major long-term demand driver.
Blue Owl closed a $335M bridge refi for Wynwood Plaza and Nuveen provided $124M C-PACE — capital markets appetite for Wynwood product is deep despite broader CRE caution.
Edgewater has 1,317 multifamily units under construction (Feb 2026) with 14+ additional projects planned in Wynwood — elevated supply risk is the primary headwind to acquisition score.
Moishe Mana acquired a second Wynwood assemblage in January 2026 ($33.5M for 1.6 acres) — his portfolio concentration signals developer conviction in long-term land appreciation.
OZ program made permanent by OBBBA (July 2025). Wynwood previously attracted $2B+ in OZ investment. New designations and deal structures remain viable for qualified projects.
Wynwood Haus (224-unit, opened 2025) stabilized at 96% occupancy — strong real-world proof point for new supply absorption in the submarket.
Miami metro led all U.S. metros in private-sector job growth in August 2025. Labor force participation at 63.8% vs 62.7% national average — underlying demand fundamentals remain strong.
NRD-2 (Wynwood Norte) zoning overlay and Live Local Act together enable 27-story density on qualifying sites — regulatory environment is among the most developer-friendly in the U.S.

feature_gaps_identified.gap: No granular lis pendens count by ZIP code or neighborhood — Miami-Dade Clerk portal and PropertyShark do not offer zone-level filterable exports without a paid subscription
Building permit counts (YTD) for Wynwood/Edgewater are not accessible from City of Miami permit portal via web search — requires direct API or portal scraping
CMBS delinquency rate for Miami/Wynwood submarket requires Trepp or MSCI subscription — publicly available CMBS data is metro-level only
Agency lending volume (Fannie/Freddie) for this submarket not publicly available at zip/neighborhood level — requires CoStar or NMHC membership
Cap rate distribution by asset class (multifamily vs. mixed-use vs. office) not available without CoStar/RCA subscription — estimated from comp prices only
Rent by vintage/class (A/B/C) breakdown requires RealPage or CoStar data — estimated from asking rent spreads across listing sites
Supply pipeline absorption timeline model — need delivery schedule overlaid with demand model to forecast occupancy pressure; not available from public sources
Interactive submarket map showing transaction heat, pipeline density, and distress concentration by block — would significantly improve zone-level decision making
STR (Airbnb/VRBO) penetration rate for Edgewater waterfront units — AirDNA data not accessible without subscription; STR share of effective supply not quantifiable
